# Monorepo Setup Guide

How to set up Frontloom in a monorepo so all packages share the same frontend quality standards.

---

## Turborepo

### Install at root

```bash
# From the monorepo root
npm install frontloom --save-dev -w
# or
pnpm add frontloom --save-dev -w
```

### Option A: Shared config at root (Recommended)

Create a single `.claude/settings.json` at the monorepo root. Claude Code picks up the nearest settings file, so root-level config applies to all packages.

```
monorepo/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .claude/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ settings.json       в†ђ shared Frontloom config
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ apps/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ web/                 в†ђ Next.js app
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ admin/               в†ђ Vue app
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ docs/                в†ђ Astro site
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ packages/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ui/                  в†ђ shared components
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ utils/               в†ђ shared utilities
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ package.json
в””в”Ђв”Ђ turbo.json
```

```json
// .claude/settings.json (root)
{
  "skills": [
    "node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md"
  ]
}
```

With this setup, the core skill applies everywhere. But different apps use different frameworks вЂ” handle that per-app.

### Option B: Per-app overrides

Each app can have its own `.claude/settings.json` that adds the framework-specific skill:

```
monorepo/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .claude/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ settings.json       в†ђ core skill only
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ apps/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ web/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ .claude/
в”‚   в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ settings.json  в†ђ adds frontloom-react
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ admin/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ .claude/
в”‚   в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ settings.json  в†ђ adds frontloom-vue
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ docs/
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ .claude/
в”‚           в””в”Ђв”Ђ settings.json  в†ђ adds frontloom-astro
```

```json
// apps/web/.claude/settings.json
{
  "skills": [
    "../../node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md",
    "../../node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-react.md"
  ]
}
```

```json
// apps/admin/.claude/settings.json
{
  "skills": [
    "../../node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md",
    "../../node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-vue.md"
  ]
}
```

```json
// apps/docs/.claude/settings.json
{
  "skills": [
    "../../node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md",
    "../../node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-astro.md"
  ]
}
```

> **Note:** Paths are relative to the settings file location. With hoisted `node_modules` at root, use `../../node_modules/...` from `apps/*/`.

### Option C: CLAUDE.md per app

If you prefer keeping config in CLAUDE.md:

```markdown
<!-- apps/web/CLAUDE.md -->
## Frontend Standards

This app uses Next.js with React. Follow these skills for all frontend work:
- `../../node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md`
- `../../node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-react.md`

References are in `../../node_modules/frontloom/references/`.
```

---

## pnpm Workspaces

Same approach as Turborepo. The only difference is `node_modules` location.

### With hoisting (default)

```bash
pnpm add frontloom --save-dev -w
```

`node_modules` is at root. Paths same as Turborepo.

### With strict isolation (`node-linker=isolated`)

Each package has its own `node_modules`. Install per-package or use `.pnpmfile.cjs` to inject:

```bash
# Install in specific workspace
pnpm add frontloom --save-dev --filter @myorg/web
```

```json
// apps/web/.claude/settings.json
{
  "skills": [
    "node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md",
    "node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-react.md"
  ]
}
```

---

## Nx

### Install

```bash
npm install frontloom --save-dev
```

### Project-level configuration

Nx recommends per-project configuration. Add `.claude/settings.json` per project:

```
nx-workspace/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ apps/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ web/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ .claude/settings.json
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ mobile-web/
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ .claude/settings.json
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ libs/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ui/
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ .claude/settings.json
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ node_modules/
в””в”Ђв”Ђ nx.json
```

```json
// apps/web/.claude/settings.json
{
  "skills": [
    "../../node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md",
    "../../node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-react.md"
  ]
}
```

```json
// libs/ui/.claude/settings.json вЂ” shared component library
{
  "skills": [
    "../../node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md",
    "../../node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-react.md"
  ]
}
```

---

## Shared Component Library

If you have a shared `packages/ui/` used across apps:

```json
// packages/ui/.claude/settings.json
{
  "skills": [
    "../../node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md",
    "../../node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-react.md"
  ]
}
```

Add a `CLAUDE.md` in the shared package to document any component library-specific patterns:

```markdown
<!-- packages/ui/CLAUDE.md -->
## Component Library Standards

This is a shared component library consumed by multiple apps.

Additional rules beyond Frontloom:
- All components must be exported from `src/index.ts`.
- All components must have a corresponding `.stories.tsx` file.
- Use CSS Modules for styling вЂ” no Tailwind in the shared library.
- All props must be documented with JSDoc comments.
- Components must work in both light and dark themes.
```

---

## Copy-Based Setup (No node_modules paths)

If managing relative `node_modules` paths across a monorepo is too fragile, copy Frontloom files to a shared location:

```bash
# Create shared config at root
mkdir -p .claude/skills .claude/references

# Copy once (or add to a setup script)
cp node_modules/frontloom/skills/* .claude/skills/
cp node_modules/frontloom/references/* .claude/references/
```

```json
// Any app's .claude/settings.json
{
  "skills": [
    "../../.claude/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md",
    "../../.claude/skills/frontloom-react.md"
  ]
}
```

Add a `postinstall` script to keep files in sync:

```json
// package.json (root)
{
  "scripts": {
    "postinstall": "cp -r node_modules/frontloom/skills/ .claude/skills/ && cp -r node_modules/frontloom/references/ .claude/references/"
  }
}
```

---

## Tips

### Git-ignore or commit?

If you copy files to `.claude/`:
- **Commit** the `.claude/settings.json` files (so all developers use the same config).
- **Git-ignore** the copied skill/reference files if you use `postinstall` to sync them.
- **Commit** the copied files if you want them available without `npm install`.

```gitignore
# .gitignore
.claude/skills/
.claude/references/
!.claude/settings.json
```

### Different frameworks, same team

In monorepos with mixed frameworks, the core skill ensures consistent quality across all apps вЂ” same accessibility, security, TypeScript, and UX standards regardless of whether the app is React, Vue, or Astro.

### Shared references

References don't need per-app config. Skills tell Claude when to read them, and the paths in the skill files point to `references/` relative to the skill location. As long as the `references/` folder is next to the `skills/` folder, everything works.
